skill.name: journal-matchmaker
skill.description: Analyzes academic paper abstracts to recommend optimal journals for submission, considering impact factors, scope alignment, and domain expertise.
skill.summary: Matches research paper abstracts to the most suitable academic journals based on scope, impact factor, and field alignment.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 65 static analysis findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The 22 Weak cryptographic algorithm findings matched the word algorithm in field detection keywords and documentation strings, not actual cryptographic code. The 37 Ruby/shell backtick execution findings were standard Markdown inline code formatting, not shell command execution. System reconnaissance, path traversal, and hardcoded URL findings referenced documentation text and security checklist items describing defensive patterns. The skill is a legitimate academic journal matching tool with no network calls, no credential access, and no data exfiltration. Minor risk exists from the file read feature (--file flag) which is intentional functionality for reading abstract files from disk.

low_findings.title: File read via --file flag allows arbitrary file access
low_findings.description: The --file flag in scripts/main.py allows reading any file path provided by the user. This is intentional functionality for reading abstract files but could be misused for unauthorized file access. Risk is limited in sandboxed execution environments.

content.user_title: Find the Best Journal for Your Research Paper
content.value_statement: Researchers waste time submitting to journals that reject their work due to poor scope fit. This skill analyzes your paper abstract and recommends the most suitable journals based on research field, impact factor, and scope alignment.
actual_capabilities: Analyze a paper abstract and extract key research terms and methodology
Detect the primary research field from abstract content
Match abstract content against a database of journal scopes and aims
Filter journal recommendations by minimum and maximum impact factor
Rank journals by relevance score with detailed match reasoning
Output results in table, JSON, or markdown format
limitations: Journal database is static and should be updated quarterly for accuracy
Impact factor data reflects historical values, not current rankings
Cannot access external databases or live journal citation data
Field detection accuracy depends on abstract length and clarity

use_cases.title: Find First Publication Venue
use_cases.description: An early-career researcher submits their first manuscript abstract and receives a ranked list of suitable journals with scope fit explanation.

use_cases.title: Target High-Impact Journals
use_cases.description: A postdoctoral researcher filters recommendations by minimum impact factor to identify top-tier journals aligned with their paper topic.

use_cases.title: Survey Publishing Landscape
use_cases.description: A research group lead analyzes multiple abstracts to survey suitable publication venues across their team projects.
use_cases.target_user: Research group lead or lab director

prompt_templates.title: Basic Journal Matching
prompt_templates.prompt:
Analyze this abstract and recommend the best journals for submission:

[Paste your abstract here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick journal recommendations with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Field-Specific Matching
prompt_templates.prompt:
Analyze this abstract in the field of [research field] and recommend suitable journals:

[Paste your abstract here]

prompt_templates.title: Impact Factor Filtering
prompt_templates.prompt:
Analyze this abstract and recommend journals with an impact factor above [number]. Exclude journals with impact factor below [number]:

[Paste your abstract here]

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt:
Analyze these two abstracts and create a comparison table of recommended journals for each. Highlight journals that appear in both lists:

Abstract 1:
[Paste abstract 1]

Abstract 2:
[Paste abstract 2]
prompt_templates.scenario: Comparing publication options for multiple papers

output_examples.input: Analyze this abstract for a machine learning paper on transformer models for medical image analysis.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a ranked table showing the top 5 journals. For each journal it displays name, abbreviation, impact factor, relevance score, open access status, and key match reasons. The top recommendation is typically a field-aligned journal with scope similarity and field match clearly explained.

output_examples.input: Filter journal recommendations for an environmental science paper with minimum impact factor of 10.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a filtered list showing only journals with impact factor above 10 that match the abstract scope. Journals like Nature Climate Change (IF 29.6) and Environmental Science and Technology (IF 11.4) appear with relevance scores and specific scope match reasons.

output_examples.input: Recommend journals for a biology paper and output the results in markdown format.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a markdown-formatted report with numbered journal entries. Each entry includes full journal name, abbreviation, impact factor, publisher, open access status, relevance score, match reasons, and scope description.

best_practices: Provide the full abstract for the most accurate field detection and scope matching. Shorter text reduces matching precision.
Use the impact factor filter to narrow results when you have specific journal tier requirements. Start with a broader filter and narrow down.
Review the match reasons for each recommendation to understand why a journal was selected. This helps you evaluate fit beyond the score.
anti_patterns: Do not paste only the paper title or keywords. The skill needs the full abstract to extract meaningful research terms and methodology context.
Do not assume impact factors are current. The journal database is a snapshot and should be updated regularly for accurate rankings.
Do not use this skill for non-academic content matching. The keyword extraction and field detection are designed specifically for research paper abstracts.

faq.question: How does the skill match journals to my abstract?
faq.answer: It extracts key terms from your abstract using frequency analysis, detects the research field, then compares your abstract against journal scope descriptions using similarity scoring.

faq.question: What data sources are used for journal information?
faq.answer: The skill uses a local database of journals with metadata including impact factor, scope description, publisher, and research fields stored in reference JSON files.

faq.question: Can I filter by impact factor?
faq.answer: Yes. You can set a minimum impact factor threshold and an optional maximum impact factor to restrict recommendations to journals within your desired range.

faq.question: Does the skill require internet access?
faq.answer: No. All analysis runs locally using the bundled journal database. The skill makes no external network calls and does not access live citation data.

faq.question: How many journals are in the recommendation database?
faq.answer: The database includes over 30 major journals across multiple fields including medicine, biology, computer science, physics, chemistry, and environmental science.

faq.question: Can the skill handle abstracts from any academic field?
faq.answer: It supports 11 predefined research fields including computer science, medicine, biology, physics, chemistry, and others. Emerging fields may require manual curation.
